message.content:
POLARIS MISSION: beat ChatGPT + Gemini + FS-Researcher on deep-research reports.
  DEPTH = process ALL 346 baskets. FAITHFULNESS = context NLI + STRICT numbers.
BINDING INVARIANTS (docs/agentic_outline_redesign.md): faithfulness engine (strict_verify + NLI +
numeric + provenance) is the ONLY hard gate; external content re-enters ONLY via the fold-in seam;
computed numbers render ONLY via the verified [#calc:] lane (never [CITE:ev_xxx]); exploratory
execute_python is BARRED from rendering; weight-and-consolidate, never filter.
FENCE: work ONLY inside /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent (branch bot/outline-agent-box). NEVER touch another worktree or the
operator's /home/polaris/polaris_project monitoring dir. Report REAL output only; never fake a pass.


YOU ARE FABLE. 4 diagnostic lenses scanned "outline". Dedup by file:line,
rank most-severe-first, keep each finding's fix + acceptance. Then decide sign_off: TRUE only if
0 P0 and 0 P1. Never force-approve. FOCUS: Build the unified agentic-outline: close the compute-reachability P1, then build the toolkit. FENCE: only touch files under /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent; NEVER touch /home/polaris/polaris_project (monitoring dir). READ /workspace/POLARIS/docs/agentic_outline_redesign.md first (read-only) — verified_compute is T1 step 5, inside the toolkit. INHERITED @ 98726ca (verify it passes, it is a WIP snapshot): execute_python un-bricked; new tests. THE OPEN P1: a computed value cannot REACH the outline — summary_for_llm() (analysis_notebook.py:142) emits only 'tool [OK] (elapsed)', no markdown/stats, so the decide LLM learns nothing. Surfacing must be BOUNDED+LABELED; to RENDER a computed number it MUST route through the verified [#calc:] lane (quantified_analysis.py:617), NEVER [CITE:ev_xxx] (strict_verify (d) drops derived numbers). Then build T1 outline_toolkit.py (fetch_url reusing the ONE fold-in seam _offset_renumber:610+_stamp_and_delete:792, search_corpus, get_evidence, list_baskets, calculator, coverage_audit, preview_section_evidence, find_contradictions, corroboration_profile, verified_compute), then T2 battery. BASE GATE never relax: full 346-basket corpus, cp4_used=agentic never fallback-plain, seed-fallback on GLM-5.2 truncation. Diagnose against the REAL code + the inherited tests; the P1 (compute unreachable) is a real correctness gap Fable should rank P0/P1. Report PARTIAL honestly.

RAW FINDINGS:
[
  {
    "severity": "P1",
    "file_line": "/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/src/polaris_graph/outline/outline_agent.py:1609",
    "problem": "Render half of the compute-reachability P1 is still open: the surfacing half IS closed at 98726ca (summary_for_llm(include_results=True) digest at analysis_notebook.py:227-276, caller outline_agent.py:1174; _compute_result_check appends workspace.compute_results at :1609 with renderable=False; carried to exit as notebook_compute at :2108; inherited tests 14/14 pass). But no tool bridges the agent loop to the verified [#calc:] lane: quantified_analysis.execute_quantified_model (generator/quantified_analysis.py:650), QuantifiedResult.calc_token (:617) and bind_calc_tokens (:782) plus tradeoff_modeler's ModelSpec/whitelisted-AST validator (synthesis/tradeoff_modeler.py:135,:247) are completely unreferenced by outline_agent.py. verified_compute (redesign doc T1 step 5, doc :393-397) does not exist anywhere in src/. So every computed number terminates at the planner forever — the ONLY legitimate render lane is unreachable, and the [#calc:] invariant is unfalsifiable because nothing can ever exercise it.",
    "evidence": "grep for verified_compute across src/ returns zero hits outside docs; grep for calc_token/execute_quantified_model shows callers only inside generator/, none in outline/; outline_agent.py:1620 bar_reason string promises 'may render only via the verified [#calc:]/ModelSpec lane' — a lane the agent cannot invoke.",
    "fix": "Implement verified_compute in the new src/polaris_graph/outline/outline_toolkit.py per doc T1 step 5: tool takes named inputs each bound to an evidence span (ev_id + raw_literal + literal_start/end resolved from workspace.ev_store) plus a formula; builds a ModelSpec with SourcedInputs, calls execute_quantified_model (fail-closed on non-executing model), returns a ToolResult whose markdown carries display_value + the [#calc:model_id:spec_hash:field_id] token and NEVER a [CITE:ev_xxx] for the derived number; register it in OutlineAgent._build_registry.",
    "acceptance": "New unit test: verified_compute over fixture evidence rows returns success ToolResult whose markdown contains '[#calc:' and no '[CITE:' for the derived value, with a persisted QuantifiedResult; a companion assert shows execute_python stats still land renderable=False. registry.get_tool('verified_compute') is not None after _build_registry()."
  },
  {
    "severity": "P1",
    "file_line": "/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/src/polaris_graph/outline/outline_agent.py:1040",
    "problem": "outline_toolkit.py does not exist and ALL nine non-compute T1 tools are missing. _build_registry() = build_default_registry() (the 8 analysis tools, tool_registry.py:690) + inspect_basket + search_more_evidence + update_outline (+ finish_outline pseudo-action). Missing: fetch_url, search_corpus, get_evidence, list_baskets, calculator, coverage_audit, preview_section_evidence, find_contradictions, corroboration_profile. Concrete consequences on a 346-basket run: the agent cannot read the FULL text of any ev_id (get_evidence), cannot keyword/BM25-search the folded ev_store (search_corpus), cannot enumerate baskets (list_baskets — inspect_basket at :873 requires already knowing a basket_id), has no deterministic never-LLM coverage accounting (coverage_audit) so section-coverage judgments ride entirely on the LLM gap checklist, and has no arithmetic primitive (calculator) despite the whitelisted-AST evaluator already existing at tradeoff_modeler.py:135/:247. find_contradictions exists only as the cross-run memory hook (memory/verified_claim_graph.py:229), not the per-basket corpus tool the doc specifies (doc :102).",
    "evidence": "grep -rn 'fetch_url|search_corpus|list_baskets|coverage_audit|preview_section_evidence|corroboration_profile|verified_compute' src/ --include='*.py' hits only unrelated code (utils/ingest.py:2110 fetch_url helper, frame_fetcher._fetch_url_pattern:1187, verified_claim_graph:229); no register_outline_toolkit, no src/polaris_graph/outline/outline_toolkit.py.",
    "fix": "Create src/polaris_graph/outline/outline_toolkit.py with register_outline_toolkit(registry, workspace, agent_model, deadline) implementing the nine tools per doc :377-383 (calculator wraps tradeoff_modeler's AST evaluator; coverage_audit is pure-Python accounting over workspace.baskets/outline_draft; get_evidence/search_corpus/list_baskets read workspace.ev_store/baskets); call it at the end of OutlineAgent._build_registry().",
    "acceptance": "After OutlineAgent._build_registry(), set(registry names) is a superset of {fetch_url, search_corpus, get_evidence, list_baskets, calculator, coverage_audit, preview_section_evidence, find_contradictions, corroboration_profile, verified_compute}; pytest tests/polaris_graph/test_outline_toolkit.py passes including calculator AST-rejection cases (e.g. '__import__(\"os\")' rejected) and the fold-in id-collision assert."
  },
  {
    "severity": "P1",
    "file_line": "/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/src/polaris_graph/outline/outline_agent.py:756",
    "problem": "The fold-in seam is not factored as a reusable unit, so fetch_url (doc :32, :380-382 — 'fetch_url MUST reuse _offset_renumber + _stamp_and_delete') cannot be built without creating a SECOND seam. _offset_renumber (:620) and _stamp_and_delete (:802) are module-private helpers, but the actual fold sequence — existing_urls dedup (:757-763), next_ev_offset (:765), _offset_renumber (:767), _stamp_and_delete (:769-771), the ev_store insert loop (:772-773), and the disclosure (:774-782) — is inlined inside _tool_search_more_evidence. A fetch_url implementation would have to copy-paste that block; any later drift between the two copies (e.g. one gains a new screen, the other doesn't) lets externally fetched rows enter ev_store unstamped or un-renumbered, violating the binding invariant that external content re-enters ONLY via the one fold-in seam.",
    "evidence": "outline_agent.py:772 'for row in kept_rows: workspace.ev_store[row[\"evidence_id\"]] = row' is the only external-row write path and it lives inside _tool_search_more_evidence; nothing exports the composed sequence.",
    "fix": "Extract a single shared async fold helper (doc suggests outline/_fold_in.py or outline_toolkit.py): fold_rows_into_workspace(workspace, rows, research_question, agent_model, label) performing dedup -> offset -> _offset_renumber -> _stamp_and_delete -> ev_store insert -> disclose, re-point _tool_search_more_evidence to it, then implement fetch_url as single-URL fetch (reuse src/utils/ingest.fetch_url:2110 or frame_fetcher._fetch_url_pattern:1187) -> row shaping -> the SAME fold call.",
    "acceptance": "grep shows exactly one code path writing external rows into workspace.ev_store (the shared fold helper); a test asserts a fetch_url'd row receives an offset-renumbered ev_id, passes the stamp screen, appears in workspace.disclosures, and that an injected id collision raises AssertionError (the :625 fail-loud seam)."
  },
  {
    "severity": "P2",
    "file_line": "/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/src/polaris_graph/tools/tool_registry.py:42",
    "problem": "ToolDefinition has only name/description/requires_data/requires_llm/parameters/execute — no tags/core fields, which doc T1 step 1 (:385) requires so the decide prompt can list core tools in full and group the rest (doc :156-158). Once the ~10 T1 tools land, the flat available_tools() listing in the decide prompt (outline_agent.py:1163-1165) grows unbounded and unclassified, bloating every decide turn on GLM-5.2 and burying the outline verbs among analysis tools.",
    "evidence": "tool_registry.py:42-50 dataclass fields; outline_agent.py:1163 'available = self.registry.available_tools(True) + [_FINISH_ACTION]' renders one flat list.",
    "fix": "Add tags: list[str] = field(default_factory=list) and core: bool = True to ToolDefinition (defaults keep the existing 12 registrations byte-compatible); set tags/core in register_outline_toolkit; render the decide prompt as core-tools-in-full + one grouped line per tag family.",
    "acceptance": "ToolDefinition(name='x', description='d', requires_data=False, requires_llm=False, tags=['corpus'], core=False) constructs; a decide-prompt unit test asserts core tools appear with full descriptions and non-core tools appear grouped; existing 14 outline tests still pass."
  },
  {
    "severity": "P2",
    "file_line": "/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/src/polaris_graph/tools/analysis_notebook.py:77",
    "problem": "Edge in the new digest (the P1 surfacing fix): in _result_digest_lines, when the FIRST stats pair alone exceeds the budget, the guard 'len(...) > budget and shown' is falsy (shown is empty) so the pair is appended anyway; the for-else then calls _emit('stats: ...') which fails the budget check and drops the ENTIRE stats line silently — no '+N omitted' disclosure. Related: when pair 1 overflows with more pairs following, the emitted '(+N more omitted)' line itself contains the oversized pair and exceeds the budget. This recreates, for that step, exactly the undisclosed-unreachable-result failure the digest exists to end.",
    "evidence": "Trace with statistics={'table': '<700-char string>'} and PG_NOTEBOOK_SUMMARY_RESULT_CHARS=600: pairs=[p1]; i=0, shown=[] -> guard falsy -> shown=[p1]; else-branch _emit returns False; out has no stats line and no omission marker.",
    "fix": "Before the loop, handle the single-oversized-pair case: truncate the value to fit and disclose, e.g. emit f'stats: {k}={str(v)[:budget-len(k)-20]}… (truncated to budget)'; also cap the '(+N more omitted)' line by truncating shown pairs to the budget.",
    "acceptance": "New unit test: a ToolResult with one 700-char stat value and budget 600 produces a digest line containing the stat key and a truncation marker (not an empty stats section); existing digest tests stay green."
  }
]

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch
